notes: kiro-cli variant: one turn runs, the session exits cleanly (/quit → process_exited + session delete), then is relaunched via `kiro-cli chat --resume-id <uuid>` in the SAME cwd. kiro APPENDS to the SAME <uuid>.jsonl, so the daemon-side session_id is STABLE across both process lifetimes — ONE session id, TWO PIDs. The clean exit deletes the session; resume activity arriving after the daemon's deletedCooldown (10s; recipe sleeps 12s) re-creates the SAME session row from transcript activity (the --continue re-creation path). v1_session_birth anchors UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal the birth; the turn/exit/resume phases pin to v1_turn_start via same_session_as. Observations: kiro's JSONL has no model/token/cost, and the v1 adapter does not read the metering sidecar — so model is unknown, cost and tokens are zero. BACKFLOW (recording 2026-06-05): live verify proved the daemon emits an EMPTY model for kiro-cli, not the placeholder "unknown" — model assertion removed to match every recorded kiro-cli cell (JSONL has no model field; v1 adapter reads no metering sidecar).

text: First launch: session appears in ready
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit (/quit) after the first turn: process_exited for the session
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — `kiro-cli chat --resume-id <uuid>` reopens the SAME <uuid>.jsonl, so the SAME session id re-binds to a new PID in ready after the cooldown
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' — the second turn drives the SAME session to working
Second turn completes back to ready on the SAME session — each lifetime's arc is independently correct
